rule.name: Azure VM with Password Authentication Enabled
rule.rule_desc: Detects Azure Linux VMs with password authentication enabled instead of SSH keys.
rule.vuln_desc: Password authentication exposes VMs to brute-force attacks. SSH key-based authentication is significantly more resistant to credential stuffing and brute-force attempts.
rule.developer: Set disable_password_authentication = true and use admin_ssh_key blocks with RSA 4096-bit or Ed25519 keys.
rule.reviewer: Confirm all Linux VMs use SSH key authentication exclusively. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Azure Storage Account Allows Public Blob Access
rule.rule_desc: Detects Azure Storage Accounts configured to allow public blob access.
rule.vuln_desc: Public blob access allows unauthenticated read of container contents, leading to data exposure of sensitive files, backups, and configuration data.
rule.developer: Set allow_nested_items_to_be_public = false at the storage account level. Use SAS tokens or Azure AD authentication for controlled access.
rule.reviewer: Confirm no storage accounts allow public blob access unless explicitly serving public-facing content. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Azure Storage Account Without Secure Transfer Required
rule.rule_desc: Detects storage accounts that allow unencrypted HTTP access.
rule.vuln_desc: Allowing HTTP traffic to Azure Storage enables data interception via MITM attacks on storage API calls, including credential exfiltration from SAS token traffic.
rule.developer: Set enable_https_traffic_only = true (default) or min_tls_version = "TLS1_2" to enforce encrypted connections.
rule.reviewer: Confirm all storage accounts enforce HTTPS. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Azure NSG Rule Allows All Inbound Traffic
rule.rule_desc: Detects Azure NSG rules that allow all inbound traffic from any source.
rule.vuln_desc: Open inbound NSG rules expose Azure resources to internet-wide scanning and exploitation across all ports from any IP address.
rule.developer: Restrict source_address_prefix to known IP ranges or service tags. Define explicit port ranges and protocol restrictions.
rule.reviewer: Confirm open inbound rules are intentional and that resources have application-layer controls. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Azure SQL Server Allows All Azure Services
rule.rule_desc: Detects Azure SQL firewall rules using the 0.0.0.0/0.0.0.0 pattern that allows all Azure services to connect.
rule.vuln_desc: The "Allow access to Azure services" firewall rule allows any Azure-hosted service (including from other tenants) to connect to the SQL server, not just services in your own tenant.
rule.developer: Use Private Endpoints for SQL connectivity instead of firewall rules. If firewall rules are necessary, restrict to explicit IP ranges.
rule.reviewer: Confirm the 0.0.0.0/0.0.0.0 firewall rule is not used for production SQL servers. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.

rule.name: Azure SQL Transparent Data Encryption Disabled
rule.rule_desc: Detects Azure SQL databases without Transparent Data Encryption configured.
rule.vuln_desc: Without TDE, Azure SQL database files, backups, and transaction logs are stored unencrypted, exposing data if storage-level access is gained.
rule.developer: Enable TDE using azurerm_mssql_server_transparent_data_encryption. Use customer-managed keys in Azure Key Vault for additional control.
rule.reviewer: Verify all SQL databases have TDE enabled. This can be confirmed as an issue when the matched path is reachable and required controls are missing, weak, or inconsistent.
